response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems software engineer with a long track record in web, mobile, and low-latency backend work, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the core demands of this role. The Principal Performance Architect position requires deep SoC and IP modeling expertise, GPU architecture knowledge, SIMD/MIMD familiarity, and hands-on deep learning workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest analog to performance engineering is low-latency async I/O work in DLang at Sociomantic, which is firmly in the software/networking domain rather than hardware or AI accelerator design.
strengths: Demonstrated performance-conscious software engineering: low-latency async I/O handling millions of HTTP requests per second using DLang at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
BS Computer Science from IIT Bombay with 13+ years of professional experience, satisfying the educational and experience threshold for the role
Exposure to embedded systems, robotic arm software, and C/C++ components at National Instruments, showing some ability to work closer to the hardware layer
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD paradigms — the foundational technical pillars of the role
No background in deep learning, neural network training or inference optimization, or AI accelerator hardware of any kind
Entire career trajectory is in web, full-stack, and backend software engineering with no movement toward hardware-software co-design or cloud infrastructure for AI workloads

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
Low-latency systems performance engineering in DLang
